reviewOpeners: A bold statement from start to finish.
An unexpected gem hidden in the tracklist.
This one sneaks up on you slowly.
Easily a standout moment on the record.
A track that refuses to sit still.
Quietly devastating in the best way.
reviewBodies: The production feels both intimate and enormous, with layers that reveal themselves on every listen.
Rhythms tumble over one another while the melody keeps a steady, hypnotic course.
Vocals drift just above the mix, half-whispered and entirely magnetic.
The bassline does most of the heavy lifting, anchoring an otherwise weightless arrangement.
It builds patiently before collapsing into a chorus that lingers long after it ends.
There is a warmth to the recording that feels like an old photograph come to life.
Every instrument seems to be in conversation, trading space without ever crowding.
The hook is deceptively simple, the kind you find yourself humming hours later.
